name: spawn_actor
description:
PREFERRED: Use this tool to add actors to the scene. Do not write Python scripts â€” call this tool directly.

Spawn a new actor in the current level. Use this to add objects like lights, meshes, characters, or custom Blueprints to your scene.

Common class paths:
- 'PointLight' or '/Script/Engine.PointLight' - Point light source
- 'SpotLight' - Spotlight source
- 'DirectionalLight' - Directional/sun light
- 'StaticMeshActor' - Static mesh placeholder
- 'CameraActor' - Camera
- 'PlayerStart' - Player spawn point
- '/Game/Blueprints/BP_MyActor' - Custom Blueprint actors

Returns: Actor name, class, label, and spawn location.

parameters.name: class
parameters.description: The class path to spawn (e.g., '/Script/Engine.PointLight' or 'StaticMeshActor')

parameters.name: name
parameters.description: Optional name for the spawned actor

name: get_level_actors
description:
PREFERRED: Use this tool to discover what actors exist in the current level.

Query actors in the current level with optional filtering. By default returns brief info (name, label, class). Set brief=false for full transform data.

Filter examples:
- class_filter='PointLight' - Find all point lights
- class_filter='StaticMeshActor' - Find all static meshes
- name_filter='Player' - Find actors with 'Player' in name

Returns: Array of actors. Use offset/limit for pagination on large levels.

parameters.name: class_filter
parameters.description: Optional class name to filter actors (e.g., 'StaticMeshActor', 'PointLight')

parameters.name: name_filter
parameters.description: Optional substring to filter actors by name

parameters.name: brief
parameters.description: Return brief info (name/label/class only). Set false for full transform data (default: true)

parameters.name: limit
parameters.description: Maximum number of actors to return (1-1000, default: 25)

parameters.name: offset
parameters.description: Number of actors to skip for pagination

name: set_property
description:
Set any property value on an actor, including component properties.

This is a powerful tool for modifying actor settings that aren't covered by other tools. Use dot notation to access nested properties and components.

Property path examples:
- 'bHidden' - Actor visibility
- 'Tags' - Actor tags array
- 'LightComponent.Intensity' - Light intensity
- 'LightComponent.LightColor' - Light color {R, G, B, A}
- 'StaticMeshComponent.RelativeScale3D' - Mesh scale
- 'RootComponent.RelativeLocation' - Root position

Value types: strings, numbers, booleans, objects (FVector, FRotator, FLinearColor), arrays.

Returns: Confirmation of property change.

parameters.name: actor_name
parameters.description: The name of the actor to modify

parameters.name: value
parameters.description: The value to set (type depends on property)

name: run_console_command
description:
Execute an Unreal Engine console command.

Console commands provide access to engine features, debugging tools, and configuration.

Useful commands:
- 'stat fps' - Show FPS counter
- 'stat unit' - Show frame timing
- 'show collision' - Toggle collision visualization
- 'show bounds' - Toggle bounding box display
- 'r.SetRes 1920x1080' - Set resolution
- 'slomo 0.5' - Slow motion (PIE only)
- 'ce MyEvent' - Call custom event

Note: Some commands only work in Play-In-Editor (PIE) mode.

Returns: Command execution confirmation.

parameters.name: command
parameters.description: The console command to execute (e.g., 'stat fps', 'show collision')

name: delete_actors
description:
Delete actors from the current level. WARNING: This is destructive and cannot be undone via MCP.

Deletion modes (use one):
- actor_name: Delete a single actor by name
- actor_names: Delete multiple actors by name array
- class_filter: Delete ALL actors of a specific class (use with caution!)

Best practice: Use get_level_actors first to verify which actors will be deleted.

Returns: List of deleted actor names and count.

parameters.name: actor_names
parameters.description: Array of actor names to delete

parameters.name: actor_name
parameters.description: Single actor name to delete (alternative to actor_names)

parameters.name: class_filter
parameters.description: Delete all actors matching this class name

name: move_actor
description:
Transform an actor's location, rotation, and/or scale.

Supports both absolute positioning (relative=false) and incremental changes (relative=true).
Only specify the transform components you want to change - others remain unchanged.

Examples:
- Move to position: location={x:100, y:200, z:0}
- Rotate 90 degrees: rotation={yaw:90}, relative=true
- Scale up 2x: scale={x:2, y:2, z:2}
- Move forward 50 units: location={x:50}, relative=true

Returns: Actor's new transform (location, rotation, scale).

parameters.name: actor_name
parameters.description: The name of the actor to transform

parameters.name: location
parameters.description: New location {x, y, z}. Omit to keep current.

parameters.name: rotation
parameters.description: New rotation {pitch, yaw, roll}. Omit to keep current.

parameters.name: scale
parameters.description: New scale {x, y, z}. Omit to keep current.

parameters.name: relative
parameters.description: If true, values are added to current transform instead of replacing

name: get_output_log
description:
Retrieve recent entries from the Unreal Engine output log.

Essential for debugging and monitoring engine activity. Use filters to focus on specific issues.

Common filters:
- 'Error' - Show only errors
- 'Warning' - Show warnings
- 'LogTemp' - Show UE_LOG(LogTemp, ...) output
- 'LogBlueprint' - Blueprint-related messages
- 'LogScript' - Script compilation messages
- 'LogActor' - Actor lifecycle messages

Returns: Array of log entries with timestamp, category, verbosity, and message.

parameters.name: lines
parameters.description: Number of recent lines to return (default: 100, max: 1000)

name: execute_script
description:
Execute custom scripts in Unreal Engine with user permission.

IMPORTANT: Only use this tool when no dedicated MCP tool can accomplish the task. Prefer specific tools first: spawn_actor, move_actor, set_property, blueprint_modify, open_level, asset_search, etc. Scripts require user approval and are slower than dedicated tools.

Script types:
- 'cpp': C++ via Live Coding (auto-retries on compile failure)
- 'python': Python script (requires Python plugin)
- 'console': Console command batch
- 'editor_utility': Editor Utility Widget/Blueprint

IMPORTANT: Include @Description in script header for history tracking:
/** @UnrealClaude Script\n * @Description: What this script does */

Returns: Script execution result, output, and any errors.

parameters.name: script_content
parameters.description: The script code. MUST include @Description in header comment.

name: cleanup_scripts
description:
Remove all Claude-generated scripts and clear execution history.

WARNING: This is a destructive operation that permanently deletes generated script files.

Use this to:
- Clean up before committing to version control
- Remove temporary scripts after debugging
- Reset script history for a fresh session

Affected locations:
- Generated C++ scripts in Source/UnrealClaude/Generated/
- Script execution history records

Returns: Count of deleted files and cleared history entries.

name: get_script_history
description:
Retrieve history of previously executed scripts.

Use this to understand what scripts have been run in this session. Helpful for context restoration and debugging.

Returns for each script:
- Script type (cpp, python, console, editor_utility)
- Filename (for file-based scripts)
- Description (from @Description header)
- Execution timestamp
- Success/failure status
- Error message (if failed)

Note: Full script content is NOT returned for security - only metadata.

parameters.name: count
parameters.description: Number of recent scripts to return (default: 10, max: 50)

name: capture_viewport
description:
Capture a screenshot of the active viewport.

Captures the current view from either Play-In-Editor (if running) or the active editor viewport. Useful for visual verification of scene changes.

Output: 1024x576 JPEG image encoded as base64 string.

Use cases:
- Verify actor placement after spawning/moving
- Check lighting changes
- Document scene state
- Debug visual issues

Returns: Base64-encoded JPEG image data.

name: blueprint_query
description:
Query Blueprint information (read-only).

Operations:
- 'list': Find Blueprints in project with optional filters
- 'inspect': Get detailed Blueprint info (variables, functions, parent class)
- 'get_graph': Get graph structure (node count, events, connections)

Use 'list' first to discover Blueprints, then 'inspect' or 'get_graph' for details.

Example paths:
- '/Game/Blueprints/BP_Character'
- '/Game/UI/WBP_MainMenu'
- '/Game/Characters/ABP_Hero' (Animation Blueprint)

Returns: Blueprint metadata, variables, functions, and/or graph structure.

parameters.name: blueprint_path
parameters.description: Full Blueprint asset path (required for inspect/get_graph)

parameters.name: include_variables
parameters.description: Include variable list in inspect result (default: false)

parameters.name: include_functions
parameters.description: Include function list in inspect result (default: false)

parameters.name: include_graphs
parameters.description: Include graph info in inspect result

name: blueprint_modify
description:
Create and modify Blueprints programmatically. Auto-compiles after changes.

Complexity Levels:
Level 2 (Structure): 'create', 'add_variable', 'remove_variable', 'add_function', 'remove_function'
Level 3 (Nodes): 'add_node', 'add_nodes' (batch), 'delete_node'
Level 4 (Wiring): 'connect_pins', 'disconnect_pins', 'set_pin_value'

Workflow: Use blueprint_query first to understand existing structure, then modify.

Node types: CallFunction, Branch, Event, VariableGet, VariableSet, Sequence, PrintString, Add, Subtract, Multiply, Divide

Variable types: bool, int32, float, FString, FVector, FRotator, AActor*, UObject*, etc.

Returns: Operation result with created node IDs (for subsequent connections).

parameters.name: operation
parameters.description: Operation to perform (see description for full list)

parameters.name: is_function_graph
parameters.description: True to target function graphs, false for event graphs

parameters.name: node_params
parameters.description: Node parameters: {function, target_class, event, variable, num_outputs}

parameters.name: nodes
parameters.description: Array of node specs: [{type, params, pos_x, pos_y, pin_values}]

parameters.name: connections
parameters.description: Array of connections: [{from_node, from_pin, to_node, to_pin}] (use indices or node IDs)

parameters.name: source_pin
parameters.description: Source pin name (empty for auto exec)

parameters.name: target_pin
parameters.description: Target pin name (empty for auto exec)

name: anim_blueprint_modify
description:
Comprehensive Animation Blueprint modification tool.

State Machine Operations:
- 'get_info': Overview of AnimBlueprint structure
- 'get_state_machine': Detailed state machine info
- 'create_state_machine': Create new state machine
- 'add_state', 'remove_state': Manage states
- 'set_entry_state': Set entry state for state machine
- 'add_transition', 'remove_transition': Manage transitions

Transition Configuration:
- 'set_transition_duration': Set blend duration
- 'set_transition_priority': Set evaluation priority

Condition Graph (transition logic):
- 'add_condition_node': Add logic node (TimeRemaining, Greater, Less, And, Or, Not, GetVariable)
- 'delete_condition_node', 'connect_condition_nodes', 'connect_to_result'

Node/Pin Introspection (NEW):
- 'get_transition_nodes': List all nodes in transition graph(s) with pins
- 'inspect_node_pins': Get detailed pin info for a node (types, values, connections)
- 'set_pin_default_value': Set pin value with type validation
- 'add_comparison_chain': Add GetVariable->Comparison->Result (auto-ANDs with existing)
- 'validate_blueprint': Return compile errors with full diagnostics

Visualization (NEW):
- 'get_state_machine_diagram': Generate ASCII diagram and enhanced JSON for state machine

Bulk Operations:
- 'setup_transition_conditions': Setup conditions for multiple transitions using pattern matching

AnimGraph Connection:
- 'connect_state_machine_to_output': Connect State Machine to AnimGraph Output Pose

Animation Assignment:
- 'set_state_animation': Assign AnimSequence, BlendSpace, BlendSpace1D, or Montage
- 'find_animations': Search compatible animation assets

- 'batch': Execute multiple operations atomically

parameters.name: operation
parameters.description: Operation: get_info, get_state_machine, get_state_machine_diagram, create_state_machine, add_state, remove_state, set_entry_state, add_transition, remove_transition, set_transition_duration, set_transition_priority, add_condition_node, delete_condition_node, connect_condition_nodes, connect_to_result, connect_state_machine_to_output, set_state_animation, find_animations, batch, get_transition_nodes, inspect_node_pins, set_pin_default_value, add_comparison_chain, validate_blueprint

parameters.name: is_entry_state
parameters.description: Whether this state is the entry state

parameters.name: node_type
parameters.description: Condition node type: TimeRemaining, Greater, Less, GreaterEqual, LessEqual, Equal, NotEqual, And, Or, Not, GetVariable

parameters.name: animation_type
parameters.description: Animation type: sequence, blendspace, blendspace1d, montage

parameters.name: asset_type
parameters.description: Asset type filter: AnimSequence, BlendSpace, BlendSpace1D, Montage, All

parameters.name: operations
parameters.description: Array of operations for batch mode

parameters.name: comparison_type
parameters.description: Comparison type: Greater, Less, GreaterEqual, LessEqual, Equal, NotEqual (for add_comparison_chain)

parameters.name: pin_value
parameters.description: Default value for the pin (for set_pin_default_value)

parameters.name: pin_name
parameters.description: Pin name to set value (for set_pin_default_value)

parameters.name: rules
parameters.description: Array of condition rules for setup_transition_conditions. Each rule: {match: {from, to}, conditions: [...], logic: 'AND'|'OR'}

name: asset_search
description:
PREFERRED: Use this tool instead of file-system searches to find project assets.

Search for assets in the Unreal project by class, path, or name. All filters are optional and combine with AND logic.

Filter examples:
- class_filter='Blueprint' - Find all blueprints
- class_filter='StaticMesh', path_filter='/Game/Environment/' - Static meshes in folder
- name_pattern='Player' - Assets with 'Player' in name
- path_filter='/Game/Characters/', name_pattern='Enemy' - Combined filters

Common class types: Blueprint, StaticMesh, SkeletalMesh, Texture2D, Material, MaterialInstance, AnimSequence, AnimBlueprint, SoundWave, ParticleSystem, NiagaraSystem

Returns: Array of assets with path, name, class, and package_path. Use limit/offset for pagination on large result sets.

parameters.name: path_filter
parameters.description: Path prefix to search within (e.g., '/Game/Characters/'). Searches recursively. Default: '/Game/'

parameters.name: name_pattern
parameters.description: Substring to match in asset names (case-insensitive)

parameters.name: offset
parameters.description: Number of results to skip for pagination (default: 0)

name: asset_dependencies
description:
Get all assets that a specific asset depends on (its dependencies).

Use this tool to understand what assets would need to be loaded together with a target asset. Useful for:
- Understanding asset relationships
- Checking what assets are bundled together
- Finding shared dependencies between assets

Example asset paths:
- '/Game/Blueprints/BP_Player'
- '/Game/Characters/Meshes/SK_Character'
- '/Game/Materials/M_Ground'

Returns: Array of dependency asset paths with their dependency type.

parameters.name: include_soft
parameters.description: Include soft references in addition to hard dependencies (default: true)

parameters.name: offset
parameters.description: Number of results to skip for pagination (default: 0)

name: asset_referencers
description:
Get all assets that reference a specific asset (its referencers).

Use this tool to find what would be affected if you modify or delete an asset. Essential for impact analysis before making changes.

Common use cases:
- Find all materials using a specific texture
- Find all blueprints using a specific mesh
- Check if an asset is safe to delete
- Understand how assets are connected

Example asset paths:
- '/Game/Textures/T_Icon' - Find what uses this texture
- '/Game/Meshes/SM_Rock' - Find what uses this mesh
- '/Game/Materials/M_Ground' - Find what uses this material

Returns: Array of referencer asset paths with their class type.

parameters.name: asset_path
parameters.description: Full asset path to find referencers for (e.g., '/Game/Textures/T_Icon')

parameters.name: include_soft
parameters.description: Include soft references in addition to hard references (default: true)

parameters.name: offset
parameters.description: Number of results to skip for pagination (default: 0)

name: enhanced_input
description:
Create and modify Enhanced Input assets (InputAction, InputMappingContext).

Operations:
- 'create_input_action': Create new InputAction asset
- 'create_mapping_context': Create new InputMappingContext asset
- 'add_mapping': Add key binding to mapping context
- 'remove_mapping': Remove key binding from context
- 'add_trigger': Add trigger (Hold, Tap, etc.) to mapping
- 'add_modifier': Add modifier (Negate, Scale, etc.) to mapping
- 'query_context': List all mappings in a context
- 'query_action': Get InputAction details

Value Types: 'Digital' (bool), 'Axis1D' (float), 'Axis2D' (Vector2D), 'Axis3D' (Vector)

Trigger Types: 'Pressed', 'Released', 'Down', 'Hold', 'HoldAndRelease', 'Tap', 'Pulse', 'ChordAction'

Modifier Types: 'Negate', 'Swizzle', 'Scalar', 'DeadZone'

Keys: Standard UE key names (SpaceBar, W, A, S, D, LeftMouseButton, Gamepad_FaceButton_Bottom, etc.)

Default asset path: /Game/Input/ (customizable via package_path)

parameters.name: hold_time
parameters.description: Hold duration in seconds (for Hold/HoldAndRelease triggers)

parameters.name: tap_release_time
parameters.description: Max release time for Tap trigger

parameters.name: pulse_interval
parameters.description: Interval in seconds for Pulse trigger

parameters.name: chord_action_path
parameters.description: Path to chord InputAction (for ChordAction trigger)

parameters.name: scalar
parameters.description: Scale factors: {x, y, z} for Scalar modifier

parameters.name: mapping_index
parameters.description: Index of mapping to remove (from query_context)

name: character
description:
Query and modify ACharacter actors in the current level.

Operations:
- 'list_characters': Find all characters with optional class filter
- 'get_character_info': Get mesh, animation, transform details
- 'get_movement_params': Query movement component properties
- 'set_movement_params': Modify movement values (speeds, jump, friction)
- 'get_components': List all components on a character

Characters are identified by actor name or label.

Movement properties include:
- max_walk_speed, max_acceleration, ground_friction
- jump_z_velocity, air_control, gravity_scale
- max_step_height, walkable_floor_angle
- braking_deceleration_walking, braking_friction

parameters.name: character_name
parameters.description: Character actor name or label (required for single-character ops)

name: character_data
description:
Create and manage character configuration DataAssets and stats DataTables.

DataAsset Operations:
- 'create_character_data': Create new character config asset
- 'query_character_data': Search character configs by name/tags
- 'get_character_data': Get details of specific config
- 'update_character_data': Modify existing config

DataTable Operations:
- 'create_stats_table': Create new stats DataTable
- 'query_stats_table': Get rows from stats table
- 'add_stats_row': Add new row to table
- 'update_stats_row': Modify existing row
- 'remove_stats_row': Delete row from table

Application:
- 'apply_character_data': Apply config to runtime character

Default asset path: /Game/Characters/

parameters.name: is_player_character
parameters.description: Whether this is a player character config

parameters.name: tags
parameters.description: Array of tag names for stats row

parameters.name: apply_movement
parameters.description: Apply movement stats to character (default: true)

name: material
description: Material instance creation and assignment operations for meshes and actors

parameters.name: operation
parameters.description: Operation: create_material_instance, set_material_parameters, set_skeletal_mesh_material, set_actor_material, get_material_info

parameters.name: asset_name
parameters.description: Name for the new material instance asset (for create_material_instance)

parameters.name: parent_material
parameters.description: Asset path to parent material (for create_material_instance)

parameters.name: parameters
parameters.description: Material parameters to set: {scalars: {name: value}, vectors: {name: {r,g,b,a}}, textures: {name: path}}

parameters.name: material_instance_path
parameters.description: Asset path to material instance (for set_material_parameters)

parameters.name: skeletal_mesh_path
parameters.description: Asset path to skeletal mesh (for set_skeletal_mesh_material)

parameters.name: material_slot
parameters.description: Material slot index to set (for set_skeletal_mesh_material)

parameters.name: material_path
parameters.description: Asset path to material to assign (for set_skeletal_mesh_material)

parameters.name: actor_name
parameters.description: Name or label of the actor to assign material to (for set_actor_material)

name: asset
description: Generic asset operations: set properties, save, and query assets in Content Browser

parameters.name: value
parameters.description: Value to set (type must match property type)

parameters.name: mark_dirty
parameters.description: Mark the asset as dirty (default: true if save is false)

parameters.name: class_filter
parameters.description: Filter by class name (e.g., SkeletalMesh, StaticMesh, Material)

name: open_level
description:
Open, create, save, or list level maps in the Unreal Editor.

Actions:
- 'open': Load an existing map by asset path (e.g., '/Game/Maps/MyLevel')
- 'new': Create a new blank map, or from a template if 'template' is specified
- 'save_as': Save the current level to a specified path (e.g., '/Game/Maps/MyLevel')
- 'list_templates': List all available map templates

The editor will prompt to save unsaved changes before switching levels.

Returns: The loaded map name and world info, save result, or template list.

parameters.name: level_path
parameters.description: Asset path of the level to open (required for 'open' action, e.g., '/Game/Maps/MyLevel')

parameters.name: template
parameters.description: Template name for 'new' action (omit for blank map). Use 'list_templates' to see available names.

parameters.name: save_current
parameters.description: For 'new' action: whether to prompt to save the current level first (default: true). The 'open' action always uses the engine's built-in save prompt.

parameters.name: save_path
parameters.description: Asset path to save the level to (required for 'save_as' action, e.g., '/Game/Maps/MyLevel')

name: task_submit
description:
Submit an MCP tool for async background execution.

Use this for long-running operations that might timeout with synchronous execution. Returns a task_id that you can use with task_status and task_result to track progress and retrieve results.

Workflow:
1. Call task_submit with tool name and parameters
2. Poll task_status with the returned task_id
3. When status is 'completed', call task_result to get output

Example:
  task_submit(tool_name='asset_search', params={class_filter: 'Blueprint'})
  -> Returns: {task_id: '...'}
  task_status(task_id='...')
  -> Returns: {status: 'running', progress: 50}
  task_result(task_id='...')
  -> Returns: {success: true, data: {...}}

parameters.name: tool_name
parameters.description: Name of the MCP tool to execute asynchronously

parameters.name: params
parameters.description: Parameters to pass to the tool (same as calling the tool directly)

name: task_status
description:
Get the status of an async task.

Returns the current status, progress, and timing information for a task.

Status values:
- 'pending': Task is queued but not yet started
- 'running': Task is currently executing
- 'completed': Task finished successfully
- 'failed': Task encountered an error
- 'cancelled': Task was cancelled
- 'timed_out': Task exceeded its timeout

For completed tasks, use task_result to get the full output.

name: task_result
description:
Get the result of a completed async task.

Returns the full result data including success/failure status, message, and output data.
Only works for tasks in a terminal state (completed, failed, cancelled, timed_out).

For pending or running tasks, this will return an error - use task_status to poll.

name: task_list
description:
List all async tasks in the queue.

Returns task IDs, tool names, status, and timing information for all tasks. Useful for monitoring and debugging async operations.

Options:
- include_completed: Whether to include finished tasks (default: true)
- limit: Maximum number of tasks to return (default: 50)

parameters.name: limit
parameters.description: Maximum number of tasks to return (default: 50)

name: task_cancel
description:
Cancel an async task.

For pending tasks: Immediately marks the task as cancelled.
For running tasks: Requests cancellation - the task will be cancelled at the next opportunity.
For completed tasks: Returns an error (cannot cancel completed tasks).

Note: Some tools may not check for cancellation during execution, so running tasks may still complete before the cancellation takes effect.
